items.headline: City Council reviews youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: San José is assessing its Children and Youth Services Master Plan alongside updates to youth empowerment programs and library facilities. The council examined the Safe Summer Initiative Grant and youth intervention services to evaluate how well the city is serving young residents.

items.headline: Development project at 333 Franklin Street hearing postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been postponed from April 8 and will be rescheduled for a later date. Residents who wanted to attend or comment on this project should watch for a new notice with the updated hearing date.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The City Council approved the Town Manager to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the available agenda information. Residents may want to follow up on what infrastructure or development project this contract covers.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with new consultant agreement
items.summary: The city approved a third amendment to its contract with DKS Associates for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, indicating continued work on this intersection safety improvement. The project aims to better manage traffic flow where the rail line crosses Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: City reveals updates to Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council received an update on Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, including revised scoring criteria and plan edits that will guide how Cupertino prioritizes biking and pedestrian infrastructure projects. This plan affects how city resources are allocated for safe walking and cycling routes across Cupertino.

items.headline: Council votes to change vacancy filling process
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled and reduce certain requirements. This could change how quickly the city can fill empty council seats in the future.

items.headline: New 65-unit senior housing complex approved on East St John
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments will add 65 affordable housing units for seniors at 678 E St John St, addressing San José's critical shortage of age-appropriate housing. At nearly $9 million, this is the largest residential project in this permit batch.

items.headline: Trimble Summerhill adds 6 new townhomes near Seely Avenue
items.summary: A new 6-plex townhome development is underway at 0 Seely Av as part of the broader Trimble Summerhill neighborhood expansion. This represents continued mid-density infill development in an area seeing significant new construction.
